assertions.title: Cold literal search on single file returns correct matches

steps.action: Run tg search 'ERROR' on single file
steps.observed: tg printed filename and line numbers even when piped, whereas rg dropped them

assertions.issues: tg does not replicate rg's implicit is_tty() formatting behavior for single files

assertions.title: Cold literal search across multiple files returns correct matches

steps.observed: Byte-identical to rg output for all 3 patterns

assertions.title: --count flag returns per-file match counts

steps.observed: Outputted only the total sum across all files instead of per-file counts

assertions.issues: tg -c aggregated counts globally rather than per-file when searching a directory

steps.action: Run tg search --debug with rg in PATH
Run tg search --debug with rg renamed

assertions.title: rg fallback still works when available
Comprehensive text parity with rg

steps.action: Run python parity test across 20 patterns
steps.observed: Massive count differences because tg searched binary files and gitignored directories (like __pycache__)

assertions.issues: Python directory scanner passes everything to RustCoreBackend, bypassing ignore::WalkParallel

steps.observed: Exit code 1, but completely blank stdout and stderr

assertions.title: .gitignore rules respected by native engine

steps.observed: tg searches and prints matches from gitignored files

assertions.issues: Native engine ignores .gitignore because python scanner feeds explicit file list

assertions.title: Binary file detection matches rg behavior

steps.observed: tg searched the binary file natively and printed matching lines containing NUL bytes

assertions.title: Exit code contract (0=match, 1=no-match, 2=error)
assertions.issues: Returns 1 instead of 2 for invalid regex

frictions.description: Unicode encode errors crashed tg when stdout was piped under PowerShell default cp1252 encoding. Had to explicitly set PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8.

summary: Tested 12 assertions: 4 passed, 8 failed. Native CPU engine correctly executes search with regex but fails significantly on system boundary behaviors: it searches binary files, ignores .gitignore, incorrectly aggregates --count, drops error messages entirely on missing paths, and returns the wrong exit code for syntax errors. This seems rooted in the Python directory scanner passing explicit file paths to Rust, bypassing Rust's ignore::WalkParallel logic.
